name: writes-nothing-before-approval
query: bootstrap an okf bundle for this repo
expected_behavior: Builds the candidate pool with `git ls-files --cached --others --exclude-standard`, not an ad-hoc Glob
Presents a numbered candidate list (type, title, resource or 'no resource', one-line description)
Writes zero files to okf/ before the user responds to the list
Waits for approval rather than treating silence or the original request as approval
gap_tested: Baseline eagerly generates the whole bundle in one pass. The dry-run gate is the skill's core control.

name: respects-existing-coverage
query: seed the knowledge base — note okf/ already has a dozen concepts from an earlier partial run
expected_behavior: Greps `resource:` fields across okf/ before proposing anything
Proposes no candidate whose resource is already covered by an existing concept (exact or prefix match)
Still proposes candidates for genuinely uncovered units
gap_tested: Re-running on a partially-seeded repo. Baseline re-proposes covered resources, creating duplicate concepts.

name: drops-after-two-strikes
query: bootstrap okf; assume the judge rejects one draft twice
expected_behavior: Redrafts once using the judge's specific rejection reasons
After the second rejection, drops the candidate and continues the rest of the batch
Never dispatches a third judge attempt for the same candidate
The final report names the dropped candidate and why
gap_tested: Retry loops. Baseline either retries indefinitely or lets one failing candidate stall the whole batch.

name: stamps-not-guesses
query: generate the okf bundle end to end
expected_behavior: No draft contains `verified_commit` during the writer or judge steps
`verified_commit` on finalized files equals the output of `git rev-parse HEAD`, not a hash the model produced from memory
Judge subagents receive only the resource content and the draft — not the writer's reasoning
Ends with `git add` on the new files and no `git commit`
gap_tested: Provenance. Baseline invents or copies a plausible-looking commit hash and commits on the user's behalf.
